title: SpaceX Shows Investors Thinner-Than-iPhone Prototype with Own OS
summary: SpaceX has shown investors a prototype mobile phone that is thinner than an iPhone and runs a proprietary operating system, aiming to integrate Starlink satellite connectivity for mobile communications. This marks SpaceX's entry into the mobile phone market with a unique satellite-based approach, potentially disrupting traditional telecom by offering direct-to-cell satellite services without reliance on terrestrial cell towers. The prototype is reported to run on a Qualcomm Snapdragon chip and integrate technology from xAI, Elon Musk's AI company that SpaceX acquired earlier this year. Elon Musk has publicly denied the rumors of SpaceX making such a device.
context: SpaceX's Starlink division has been developing 'Direct to Cell' technology that allows standard 4G LTE phones to connect directly to Starlink satellites, bypassing ground towers. The service is currently in beta and has been used for messaging in emergencies. SpaceX President Gwynne Shotwell has mentioned potential partnerships with cellular carriers and building ground networks to support mobile services.
